result.output:
Explanation: The question refers to a 2020 study analyzing the cuticular wax (the "first line of defense") of Pinus pinaster, a Mediterranean species in the genus Pinus. According to the abstract of the study "Nonacosan-10-ol and n-Alkanes in Leaves of Pinus pinaster" [28183], nonacosan-10-ol is the most dominant compound in the leaf cuticular wax, with an average content of 77.1%. This directly answers the question regarding the most dominant compound found in the "Results and Discussion" section.
Exact Answer: nonacosan-10-ol
Confidence: 100%
